You can add records by adding entries to the /etc/hosts file in the following format:
%IP_ADDRESS% %HOSTNAME%.lab %HOSTNAME%

Example:
192.168.123.10 router1.lab router1

If you require DNS requests to be serviced from a different subnet than the one that the DNS server resides on then do the following:

1. Edit (nano or vim) /ect/init.d/dnsmasq
2. Find the line DNSMASQ_OPTS="$DNSMASQ_OPTS --local-service"
3. Remove the --local-service or comment that line out and add DNSMASQ_OPTS=""
4. Restart dnsmasq using the following command: service dnsmasq restart 

 There is an error in this Docker container, edit /etc/dnsmasq.conf, add: interface=eth0
